title: The Twenty-First Sunday after Pentecost
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
October 17, 2021 | Preacher: Katie Pearson

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This is the Twenty-First Sunday after Pentecost in Ordinary Time (the long stretch of the Christian calendar between Pentecost and Advent). The RCL readings for this Sunday were likely Job 38:1-7 (God's response to Job), Psalm 104:1-9, 24-26, 35c, Hebrews 5:1-10, and Mark 10:35-45 (the request of James and John for seats of honor). The theme centers on suffering, divine wisdom, and the paradox of seeking greatness through service rather than dominion.

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in a period of relative stability following the 79th General Convention in July 2021, which had reaffirmed orthodox theology while maintaining progressive stances on LGBTQ+ inclusion. In the Diocese of Colorado, Bishop Robert O'Neill was serving (he had been in position since 2008), and the diocese was navigating post-pandemic reopening of in-person worship. Saint John's Cathedral had reopened for full services by this date after COVID-19 restrictions.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver was experiencing unseasonably warm October weather (temperatures in the 70s-80s Fahrenheit). The week of October 17 saw continued recovery discussions following the historic Marshall Fire preparations (though the actual Marshall Fire would occur in late December 2021, not yet). Colorado's political focus was on the 2022 midterm cycle, with gubernatorial and Senate races beginning to take shape. Downtown Denver and Capitol Hill were in the early stages of post-pandemic recovery with restaurants and cultural venues reopening.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The Biden administration was navigating ongoing Afghanistan withdrawal fallout and Taliban governance. The Senate was debating the infrastructure bill (ultimately passed on November 5, 2021). COVID-19 vaccine mandates were intensifying across federal contractors and healthcare systems, creating significant political polarization. The House Select Committee investigating the January 6 Capitol riot was holding high-profile hearings. Supply chain disruptions and inflation were beginning to dominate economic news cycles.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The UN Climate Change Conference (COP26) was scheduled to begin in Glasgow on October 31, 2021, with build-up coverage dominating climate news. The Israeli-Palestinian conflict had somewhat receded from headlines following the May 2021 ceasefire, though tensions remained. The Taliban's consolidation of power in Afghanistan following the US withdrawal was an ongoing international story of significant concern.

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

America was in a transitional cultural moment: post-acute pandemic but still navigating variants and vaccine hesitancy, economic optimism mixed with inflation anxiety, and deepening political polarization. The 2021 World Series (Houston Astros vs. Atlanta Braves) was underway, with the Braves winning in early November, though this was pre-Series. Denver sports fans were focused on the Broncos' middling NFL season. There was cultural fatigue mixed with cautious hope as fall events and in-person gatherings resumed more fully.
